description: Tomato mosaic virus is a highly persistent viral disease that can cause mottled foliage, distorted growth, and reduced yield. There is no curative treatment for an infected plant.
symptoms: Light and dark green mosaic pattern on leaves

favorable_conditions.humidity: Not the primary driver of transmission
favorable_conditions.additional_conditions: Mechanical handling and contaminated tools are important transmission risks

spread: Mechanical contact with infected plant sap
Contaminated seed can contribute to introduction
risk_factors: Handling infected and healthy plants without sanitation
prevention: Use virus-free seed and healthy transplants
Avoid unnecessary handling of suspected infected plants

immediate_actions: Isolate or remove suspected infected plants
Disinfect tools used on affected plants
Avoid handling healthy plants immediately after infected plants

chemical_control.recommended: No pesticide or fungicide can cure a plant infected with Tomato mosaic virus.
chemical_control.warning: Do not recommend fungicides or antibiotics as a cure for viral infection.

severity_guidance.low: Suspected isolated infection. Confirm diagnosis and prevent mechanical spread.
severity_guidance.moderate: Several symptomatic plants. Strengthen sanitation and remove confirmed infected plants where appropriate.
severity_guidance.high: Widespread infection. Focus on preventing further transmission and improving sanitation for future crops.

farmer_advice: There is no direct cure for Tomato mosaic virus. Management should focus on prevention, sanitation, resistant varieties, and removal of confirmed infection sources.
disclaimer: Mosaic symptoms can be caused by multiple viruses or noninfectious disorders. Laboratory confirmation may be required.
